summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Daily Checks ITEM MANAGEMENT (CRUD + scope). Backend: 11/11 pytest tests pass covering GET /items (global & location-scoped), GET /items/all (admin-only), POST/PATCH/DELETE /items (admin-only gated - 401/403 without auth), scope flipping (global<->location via `scope` field), items_snapshot stored on submit, total_items reflects visible items for location at submit time, 404 on non-existent PATCH/DELETE, 422 on too-short text. Frontend (desktop 1920 + mobile 390x844): Manage tab appears for super_admin alongside Check/History/Overview; accessible without selecting a location. 15 default items show with 'All Locations' badge + Globe icon. Add Item form works (textarea + scope dropdown + location dropdown when scope=location). Created 'TEST_item_for_timperley' for Timperley location and confirmed it appeared in list with location name (list went 15->16). Edited item text + switched scope to global; save refreshed list with updated text. Deleted item; list shrunk back. Verified Check tab for Timperley = 15 items after cleanup, then created Timperley-specific item -> Check tab = 16, switched to Oakmere -> 15 (location-specific hidden). History tab: expanding entries renders items per items_snapshot with pass/fail icons. Mobile viewport: no horizontal overflow, edit/delete buttons clickable, add form renders correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: routes/daily_checks.py:50 — `_active_items_for_location` uses pymongo sync calls inside async handlers; same sync-in-async concern noted in iteration_20 still applies.

routes/daily_checks.py:92-110 create_checklist_item — next_order is computed per scope but not under a transaction/lock, so concurrent POSTs under the same scope can collide on `order` value (cosmetic only, items still save).

routes/daily_checks.py:113-140 update_checklist_item — `scope` field parsing: when `scope='location'` but `location_id` is empty string, neither branch applies and the update silently proceeds without touching scope. Consider returning 400 for `scope='location'` missing location_id.

routes/daily_checks.py:143-148 delete_checklist_item is a hard delete. Any historical daily_checks that referenced this item keep the id in their `checks` dict but the text is only preserved via items_snapshot (which older submissions pre-migration may lack). Consider soft-delete (is_active=false) to preserve referential context, or ensure ALL past entries have items_snapshot backfilled.
admin-daily-checks/index.jsx:73 fetchItems — empty catch{} swallows errors silently; user sees stale/empty items list with no feedback.
admin-daily-checks/index.jsx:83-94 handleAddItem — uses `alert()` for errors (still in code); not mobile-friendly. Consider a toast.
admin-daily-checks/index.jsx:54 useEffect([selectedLocation]) calls fetchItems which depends on selectedLocation; on initial render selectedLocation='' → fires an extra request (harmless, but noisy).
admin-daily-checks/index.jsx handleDelete — uses native window.confirm() browser prompt; functional but inconsistent with rest of app's UI patterns. Consider shadcn AlertDialog.
seed_data_creation: All TEST_ items created during backend + frontend testing were cleaned up (backend: autouse cleanup fixture deletes TEST_* items; frontend: explicit delete via UI). Leftover daily_checks entries from iteration_20 and earlier still in DB (2026-02-11 and 2026-05-02 for Timperley; 2026-03-01 TEST_with_local_item created this iteration). These are non-blocking; no delete endpoint exists for daily_checks entries.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Daily Checks item management (CRUD + scope) is fully working end-to-end. Collection daily_check_items seeded with 15 global items on boot. Admin endpoints properly role-gated. items_snapshot correctly persisted on submit. Frontend Manage tab + Check tab integration tested across multiple locations confirming scope filtering works. Known non-blocking: admin-daily-checks/index.jsx still has empty catch blocks + alert() calls + browser-native confirm prompt (carried over from iteration_20 review, still not fixed). All data-testid attributes present for Manage tab (add-item-btn, new-item-text/scope/location, save-new-item-btn, manage-item-{id}, edit-{id}, save-edit-{id}, delete-{id}).
rca of the issue: No bugs found - feature works as designed.
